records.ety: [Of Scand. origin; cf. Dan. & Sw. skum, Icel. skūm, LG. schum, D. schuim, OHG. scūm, G. schaum; probably from a root meaning, to cover. √158. Cf. Hide skin, Meerschaum, Skim, v., Sky.]

defs: The extraneous matter or impurities which rise to the surface of liquids in boiling or fermentation, or which form on the surface by other means; also, the scoria of metals in a molten state; dross.
quotes: Some to remove the scum as it did rise.

defs: refuse; recrement; anything vile or worthless.
quotes: The great and innocent are insulted by the scum and refuse of the people.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Scummed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Scumming (?).]

defs: To take the scum from; to clear off the impure matter from the surface of; to skim.
quotes: You that scum the molten lead.

defs: To sweep or range over the surface of.
quotes: Wandering up and down without certain seat, they lived by scumming those seas and shores as pirates.

defs: To form a scum; to become covered with scum. Also used figuratively.
quotes: Life, and the interest of life, have stagnated and scummed over.
